name: Higgs couplings to gauge bosons and third-generation fermions (LHC combined)
requirement: Any candidate framework must reproduce the Higgs-boson couplings to W, Z, top, bottom, tau, and (recently) charm and muon, as measured by ATLAS and CMS in their full LHC Run 2 combinations. Coupling-strength modifiers kappa_X are consistent with unity within ~7-15% per coupling: kappa_W = 1.05 +/- 0.06; kappa_Z = 0.99 +/- 0.06; kappa_t = 0.94 +/- 0.11; kappa_b = 0.89 +/- 0.10; kappa_tau = 0.93 +/- 0.07.

regime.description: Higgs scale, ~125 GeV. Sensitive to BSM Higgs sectors (extended doublets, composite Higgs, dilaton-Higgs mixing) at the few-percent level.

current_bound.value: kappa_W, kappa_Z, kappa_t, kappa_b, kappa_tau all consistent with 1 within ~10%; kappa_mu observed at ATLAS at 2 sigma; H -> c c_bar bounded |kappa_c| < 4.2
current_bound.experiment: ATLAS and CMS combined Run 2 analysis
current_bound.reference: ATLAS Collaboration, 'A detailed map of the Higgs boson interactions by the ATLAS experiment ten years after the discovery', Nature 607, 52 (2022), arXiv:2207.00092; CMS Collaboration, Nature 607, 60 (2022), arXiv:2207.00043

what_it_excludes: Constrains BSM Higgs sectors at the few-percent level: composite Higgs scenarios with f >~ 1 TeV; two-Higgs-doublet models with tan beta < 50; heavy-vector-like-quark mixing with the SM third generation. Higgs invisible decay branching < 11% (ATLAS+CMS) constrains Higgs-portal dark matter. The Higgs self-coupling lambda is currently bounded -0.6 < lambda/lambda_SM < 6.6 and will be the focus of HL-LHC.

parameterization.name: kappa-framework: kappa_X = ratio of observed to SM coupling strength to species X. Equivalently, SMEFT operators C_phi^(...) modifying Higgs production and decay rates.
parameterization.reference: LHC Higgs Cross Section Working Group, CERN-2017-002-M (2017); de Florian et al., arXiv:1610.07922.
parameterization.summary: Each kappa is fit to a global combination of Higgs production cross sections and decay branching ratios; SMEFT translation is one-to-one at leading order.

procedural.predicted_observable: Higgs production rates (ggF, VBF, VH, ttH) times decay branching ratios in each channel (gamma gamma, ZZ -> 4l, WW -> 2l 2nu, b b_bar, tau tau, mu mu, c c_bar, Z gamma).
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Each kappa within ~10% of unity; specific combinations (e.g. fermiophobic) constrained more tightly.
framework_inputs: Match the framework onto SMEFT and compute Wilson coefficients of Higgs-relevant operators (C_phi G, C_phi W, C_phi B, C_phi WB, C_phi e, C_phi u, C_phi d, ...).

primary_references.citation: ATLAS Collaboration, 'A detailed map of the Higgs boson interactions by the ATLAS experiment ten years after the discovery', Nature 607, 52 (2022)
CMS Collaboration, 'A portrait of the Higgs boson by the CMS experiment ten years after the discovery', Nature 607, 60 (2022)
